network.overall_strategy: This network follows the literature-supported multi-environment view that ATP did not arise through a single one-pot pathway. Hydrothermal settings generate reduced C1-C3 feedstocks and a proto-energetic phosphate donor pool; surface settings produce adenine, sugar fragments, ribose-stabilized intermediates, and selective nucleoside/nucleotide assembly under UV, mineral, and wet-dry cycling; biochemical/prebiotic settings complete nucleotide elaboration and the best-supported terminal step, Fe3+-assisted phosphorylation of ADP by acetyl phosphate to ATP. To address known weaknesses, the network avoids treating free-ribose formose chemistry or direct adenine + ribose condensation as the sole central route. Instead it emphasizes literature-supported selective purine nucleoside construction through ribose-1,2-cyclic phosphate and Ti-mineral-mediated ribosylation, while retaining ribose stabilization by borate and sugar-fragment chemistry as connected feeder branches. Ten distinct pathways are encoded as different validated route combinations converging on adenosine/AMP/ADP and ending either in the strongest ATP-forming route (acetyl phosphate + Fe3+ from ADP) or the weaker but reported cTMP wet-dry phosphorylation route.

reactions.name: Hydrothermal CO2 reduction to formate

conditions.other: alkaline vent conditions, pH 9-11, H2-rich

reactions.mechanism: Mineral-catalyzed hydrogenation of CO2 on iron-sulfur surfaces gives reduced C1 carbon as formate.
reactions.reasoning: Supported by alkaline hydrothermal vent simulations showing CO2 fixation with H2 on iron-sulfur minerals, including formate production under vent-like alkaline conditions; Herschy et al. (2014) and Preiner et al. (2020).

reactions.name: Hydrothermal reduction to formaldehyde

conditions.catalyst: Ni-doped mackinawite with magnetite-like hydrothermal surfaces
conditions.other: alkaline vent reactor, proton/pH gradients possible

reactions.mechanism: Partial reduction of CO2 by H2 on sulfide surfaces generates formaldehyde as a reactive carbonyl feedstock.
reactions.reasoning: Bench-top alkaline vent reactor experiments detected formaldehyde from H2 + CO2 with Ni-doped mackinawite under vent-like conditions; Herschy et al. (2014).

reactions.name: Hydrothermal acetyl phosphate generation from vent proto-metabolism

conditions.other: alkaline hydrothermal setting; connection step is speculative but motivated by acetyl-phosphate-first protoenergetics

reactions.mechanism: Reduced C1 vent carbon chemistry is coupled conceptually to phosphate activation to supply acetyl phosphate as a proto-energetic donor.
reactions.reasoning: This step is included as a necessary connector rather than a directly demonstrated single-reaction route. The review identifies acetyl phosphate as a plausible proto-ATP in hydrothermal scenarios and notes that integrating its formation geochemically remains unresolved; de Duve (1991, 1995), Lane and Martin as summarized in the review, and Pinna et al. (2022).

reactions.name: HCN photoredox synthesis of glycolaldehyde and glyceraldehyde

conditions.other: UV irradiation; literature uses Cu(I)/Cu(II) cyanide photoredox, represented here in a mineral-rich photochemical surface setting

reactions.mechanism: Photoredox homologation of cyanide-derived carbon produces C2 and C3 sugar fragments without requiring free ribose.
reactions.reasoning: Glycolaldehyde and glyceraldehyde formation from HCN under UV via copper photoredox was demonstrated by Ritson and Sutherland (2012) and integrated into cyanosulfidic networks by Patel et al. (2015). Mineral agents are included to satisfy network schema for surface settings, but the key literature support is the Cu photoredox chemistry.

reactions.name: HCN oligomerization to DAMN

conditions.other: requires concentrated HCN or concentration by freezing/evaporation; alkaline conditions favor oligomerization

reactions.mechanism: Sequential HCN oligomerization furnishes the tetramer diaminomaleonitrile as the key bottleneck intermediate.
reactions.reasoning: DAMN is the accepted critical intermediate in adenine synthesis from HCN; Sanchez, Ferris and Orgel (1967), Oró (1961), Roy et al. (2007), and Sandström et al. (2025). Mineral surfaces are used here as plausible concentrating substrates, while the core chemistry is literature-supported.

reactions.name: UV photoisomerization of DAMN to AICN

conditions.other: strong UV irradiation

reactions.mechanism: Photochemical rearrangement of DAMN furnishes 4-aminoimidazole-5-carbonitrile, a purine-ring precursor.
reactions.reasoning: Near-quantitative UV photoisomerization of DAMN to AICN is part of the accepted HCN-to-adenine route; Sanchez, Ferris and Orgel (1967), and discussed in the review with Sandström et al. (2025).

reactions.name: AICN plus HCN to adenine

conditions.other: ammonium cyanide-rich or evaporative settings; days-scale heating possible

reactions.mechanism: Fifth cyanide equivalent closes the purine ring to give adenine.
reactions.reasoning: This follows the classic Oró adenine synthesis framework via HCN pentamerization through DAMN/AICN; Oró (1961), Sanchez et al. (1967), Roy et al. (2007), Sandström et al. (2025).

reactions.name: Formamide mineral-catalyzed adenine synthesis

conditions.other: neat formamide heating

reactions.mechanism: Thermally activated formamide condensation on mineral surfaces yields purine and pyrimidine nucleobases including adenine.
reactions.reasoning: Heating neat formamide with TiO2 or montmorillonite produces nucleobases, including adenine; Saladino et al. (2003, 2004), with broader iron-sulfur/meteorite extensions in Saladino et al. (2008, 2015).

reactions.name: Cyanamide plus glycolaldehyde to 2-aminooxazole

conditions.other: neutral to mildly basic aqueous conditions

reactions.mechanism: Condensation of glycolaldehyde with cyanamide forms the heterocycle 2-aminooxazole.
reactions.reasoning: This is the entry step of the Sutherland bypass route to nucleotide chemistry; Anastasi et al. (2006) and Powner et al. (2009).

reactions.name: 2-Aminooxazole plus glyceraldehyde to ribo-aminooxazoline

conditions.other: reported 44% ribo-aminooxazoline with some arabino isomer

reactions.mechanism: C2 and C3 sugar fragments combine through aminooxazole chemistry to give a pentose-derived heterocycle.
reactions.reasoning: Ribo-aminooxazoline formation from 2-aminooxazole and glyceraldehyde was demonstrated by Anastasi et al. (2006) and underpins the Powner et al. (2009) bypass strategy.

reactions.name: Borate-stabilized pentose formation to ribose

conditions.other: alkaline evaporitic pools; borate stabilization and molybdate-guided rearrangements

reactions.mechanism: Formaldehyde and glycolaldehyde enter borate/molybdate-directed sugar chemistry yielding pentoses including ribose, while borate stabilizes the ribose furanose form.
reactions.reasoning: Ricardo et al. (2004) showed borate stabilization of ribose, Furukawa et al. (2013) supported selective stabilization, and Kim et al. (2011) showed borate+molybdate guidance of formaldehyde/glycolaldehyde mixtures toward pentoses including ribose. The review also notes the limitations of the classical formose route under mild conditions.

reactions.name: Orthophosphate activation of ribose to ribose-1,2-cyclic phosphate

conditions.other: dry-state or low-water activity conditions; cyclic phosphate used as selective glycosyl donor

reactions.mechanism: Ribose is converted into a cyclic phosphate that blocks one face and biases later glycosylation toward beta-N9 products.
reactions.reasoning: Kim and Benner (2017) identified ribose-1,2-cyclic phosphate as a selective precursor enforcing beta and N9 selectivity in adenosine precursor formation. This reaction is included as the activation step implied by that pathway.

reactions.name: Adenine plus ribose-1,2-cyclic phosphate to adenosine-2'-phosphate

conditions.other: heating at 85C; exclusive beta-anomer formation reported

reactions.mechanism: Cyclic phosphate blocks the alpha-face of ribose and directs selective N9 beta glycosylation of adenine.
reactions.reasoning: Kim and Benner (2017) showed adenine with ribose-1,2-cyclic phosphate at 85C yields adenosine-2'-phosphate in 15% yield with exclusive beta selectivity.

reactions.name: Rearrangement of adenosine-2'-phosphate to adenosine/AMP pool

conditions.other: dry-state rearrangement toward biologically relevant phosphorylation position

reactions.mechanism: The 2'-phosphate intermediate can rearrange under borate/urea-assisted conditions toward the canonical nucleotide pool, furnishing adenosine and especially 5'-phosphorylated AMP as downstream precursors.
reactions.reasoning: Kim and Benner (2017) reported that the 2'-phosphate can rearrange toward the biologically relevant 5'-phosphate using urea and borate. This connector is included to link the selective cyclic-phosphate route to the AMP/adenosine network.

reactions.name: Ti-mineral-mediated ribosylation of adenine to adenosine

conditions.temperature: noted as mineral-mediated surface heating conditions
conditions.other: reported N9-ribosylated product selectivity 13:1 over N6 products

reactions.mechanism: Titanium-containing mineral surfaces template direct ribosylation and favor N9-linked adenosine products.
reactions.reasoning: A 2022 Nature Synthesis study reported Ti-containing minerals catalyze direct ribosylation of adenine with 14% N9-ribosylated products and 13:1 N9:N6 selectivity. This route is included because the review identifies it as the strongest purely mineral-mediated direct purine ribosylation pathway.

reactions.name: Selective phosphorylation of adenosine to AMP

conditions.other: formamide medium; borate blocks 2' and 3' hydroxyl groups, giving exclusive 5'-AMP

reactions.mechanism: Borate masks vicinal cis-diols and directs phosphorylation selectively to the 5'-hydroxyl of adenosine.
reactions.reasoning: Kim et al. (2016) showed adenosine + phosphate + pyrophosphate in formamide at 90C with borate yields exclusively 5'-AMP. This avoids the poorly selective orthophosphate-only route.

reactions.name: DAP-mediated phosphorylation of adenosine to AMP

conditions.other: pH 5.5-10, room temperature to 50C; low-water paste enhances phosphorylation

reactions.mechanism: Activated phosphorus in the form of diamidophosphate phosphorylates nucleosides under mild aqueous to paste conditions.
reactions.reasoning: DAP phosphorylates nucleosides under prebiotic conditions and later work improved regioselectivity; Gibard et al. (2018) and Cruz et al. (2023). This node is represented using orthophosphate as the network phosphorus pool feeding DAP-like activated chemistry described in the review.

reactions.name: AMP to ADP phosphorylation in trimetaphosphate wet-dry cycling

conditions.other: wet-dry cycles; mixed product distributions reported

reactions.mechanism: Polyphosphate transfer from cyclic trimetaphosphate extends phosphate chains on adenylates under dehydrating conditions.
reactions.reasoning: Yamagata et al. (1991) and Yamagata/Inomata reported trimetaphosphate with Ni(II) under wet-dry conditions gives substantial adenosine phosphorylation, including ATP among products. Here it is used conservatively as a route from AMP toward ADP prior to the best-supported final ATP step.

reactions.name: ADP to ATP by acetyl phosphate and Fe3+

conditions.other: pH 5.5-6, 2-4 h, water; ~15-20% yield

reactions.mechanism: Fe3+ forms a macrochelate involving adenine N7, acetyl phosphate, and ADP beta-phosphate, enabling selective phosphoryl transfer to make ATP.
reactions.reasoning: This is the strongest direct prebiotic ATP synthesis step currently known: acetyl phosphate with Fe3+ converts ADP to ATP in water at 30C and pH 5.5-6 in ~15-20% yield, selectively for ADP; Pinna et al. (2022).

reactions.name: Direct adenosine to ATP phosphorylation by trimetaphosphate

conditions.catalyst: Ni(II)-catalyzed cTMP chemistry in volcanic polyphosphate settings
conditions.other: mixed products including 5'-ATP and cyclic AMP reported

reactions.mechanism: Polyphosphate transfer from cTMP under dehydrating conditions can directly furnish ATP among a product mixture from adenosine.
reactions.reasoning: Yamagata et al. and Inomata achieved total phosphorylation of adenosine with trimetaphosphate under Ni(II)-catalyzed wet-dry cycles, including 13% 5'-ATP. The review emphasizes that this route is less selective than the ADP + acetyl phosphate + Fe3+ pathway.

pathways.name: HCN-to-adenine, cyclic-phosphate nucleoside route, borate AMP, AcP terminal ATP
pathways.description: Classical HCN adenine synthesis converges with borate-stabilized ribose and Benner cyclic-phosphate selective purine glycosylation, then proceeds via AMP and ADP to ATP using the strongest terminal step.

pathways.name: Formamide adenine, cyclic-phosphate nucleoside route, borate AMP, AcP terminal ATP
pathways.description: Formamide bypasses HCN concentration problems for adenine production, then joins the selective ribose-1,2-cyclic phosphate route before the AcP/Fe3+ ATP step.

pathways.name: HCN-to-adenine, Ti-mineral adenosine, borate AMP, AcP terminal ATP
pathways.description: Adenine from the Oró route is ribosylated by Ti-containing minerals to avoid weak unguided glycosylation, then phosphorylated to ATP via ADP.

pathways.name: Formamide adenine, Ti-mineral adenosine, borate AMP, AcP terminal ATP
pathways.description: Formamide-derived adenine converges with borate-guided ribose chemistry and Ti-mineral ribosylation before phosphorylation to ATP.

pathways.name: HCN-to-adenine, Ti-mineral adenosine, DAP-mediated AMP, AcP terminal ATP
pathways.description: This branch uses the milder activated-phosphorus DAP phosphorylation rather than borate-formamide phosphorylation before converging on ADP and ATP.

pathways.name: Formamide adenine, Ti-mineral adenosine, DAP-mediated AMP, AcP terminal ATP
pathways.description: A formamide-origin purine route combined with Ti-mineral ribosylation and DAP phosphorylation converges on the Pinna ADP-to-ATP step.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal formaldehyde feed, HCN adenine, cyclic-phosphate route, AcP terminal ATP
pathways.description: Hydrothermal vent chemistry supplies formaldehyde to surface borate sugar chemistry, while adenine comes from HCN oligomerization; the route closes through selective glycosylation and AcP-driven ATP formation.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal formaldehyde feed, formamide adenine, Ti-mineral adenosine, AcP terminal ATP
pathways.description: This is a strongly multi-environment route: hydrothermal CO2 reduction feeds sugar chemistry, formamide gives adenine, Ti-minerals produce adenosine, and hydrothermal acetyl phosphate powers the final ATP step.

pathways.name: HCN adenine with direct cTMP ATP route
pathways.description: A shorter but less selective literature-supported branch in which adenosine is directly triphosphorylated by trimetaphosphate under wet-dry conditions.

pathways.name: Formamide adenine with direct cTMP ATP route
pathways.description: Formamide supplies adenine, borate-stabilized ribose feeds Ti-mineral adenosine formation, and volcanic trimetaphosphate chemistry gives ATP directly as a mixed phosphorylation product.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Strong literature alignment for HCN-to-adenine, Benner-type cyclic phosphate nucleoside formation, and the Fe3+/acetyl phosphate terminal ATP step. Main weakness is the borate/formose ribose branch: free ribose production from formaldehyde/glycolaldehyde remains poorly selective and is challenged by recent literature. Ribose-1,2-cyclic phosphate generation from orthophosphate is plausible as a network connector but under-specified and less directly validated than the downstream adenine coupling.
suggestions: Replace or qualify free-ribose formation as low-yield/selectivity-limited rather than a robust step
Specify mineral/activation context for ribose-1,2-cyclic phosphate formation
Clarify whether adenosine-2'-phosphate rearrangement to AMP requires urea/borate or proceeds through a mixture

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This is one of the better pathways because the formamide-to-adenine route is strongly literature-supported and avoids the HCN concentration bottleneck. The weakest element remains the explicit free-ribose branch and the under-justified conversion of ribose to ribose-1,2-cyclic phosphate. Final ATP formation from ADP via acetyl phosphate and Fe3+ is excellent and matches the strongest known experimental support.
suggestions: State TiO2 or montmorillonite catalysts explicitly for the formamide route if absent in full reaction metadata
Downgrade certainty of ribose formation and emphasize it as a feeder rather than central validated route
Add conditions for adenosine-2'-phosphate rearrangement consistent with Kim and Benner

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This route is coherent and comparatively strong because Ti-mineral-mediated adenine ribosylation is a good literature-supported alternative to direct free-base/free-ribose condensation, and the cTMP-to-ADP plus acetyl phosphate/Fe3+ terminal ATP step is plausible. The principal weakness is still dependence on free ribose from formose-like chemistry.
suggestions: Support phosphorylation of adenosine to AMP with borate/formamide or DAP-specific conditions rather than generic orthophosphate
Note that Ti-mineral ribosylation gives selective N9 products but may still require specific mineralogy and dry-state conditions
Reduce reliance on standalone ribose accumulation

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Among the cleanest pathways in the network. Formamide adenine synthesis and Ti-mineral-mediated ribosylation are both literature-grounded, and the final ADP to ATP step is the strongest part of the whole network. Remaining concern is that the route still imports free ribose from a weakly supported formose/borate step rather than using a stronger purine nucleoside assembly strategy such as FaPy chemistry.
suggestions: Substitute the ribose source with a more selective literature-backed purine nucleoside precursor route
Specify phosphorylation conditions that improve 5'-selectivity
Clarify whether AMP formed is predominantly 5'-AMP

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This is one of the strongest routes. DAP-mediated phosphorylation is much better supported than generic orthophosphate phosphorylation, Ti-mineral-mediated adenosine formation is plausible, and the terminal acetyl phosphate/Fe3+ ATP formation is highly literature-aligned. The main residual issue is the weak free-ribose feeder branch.
suggestions: Frame ribose production as a constrained, borate-stabilized minor route rather than robust output
Include explicit low-water-activity or paste conditions for DAP phosphorylation
If possible, replace free ribose with cyclic-phosphate or FaPy-like entry to improve realism

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Best overall pathway in the network. It combines a robust adenine source (formamide), selective mineral-mediated nucleoside formation, a well-supported DAP phosphorylation route, plausible cTMP-mediated AMP to ADP conversion, and the strongest known ATP-forming terminal step from ADP plus acetyl phosphate with Fe3+. The only significant weakness is the still-problematic free-ribose origin.
suggestions: Improve the sugar branch by emphasizing borate-stabilized pentose pools rather than direct ribose abundance
Explicitly annotate mineral agents and conditions for all surface steps in the full network metadata
Consider adding a caveat that chirality and ribose availability remain unresolved

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This pathway is particularly good on environmental progression because it explicitly connects hydrothermal formaldehyde production to surface sugar chemistry and then to biochemical ATP completion. However, the hydrothermal reduction of CO2 all the way to formaldehyde is less established than formate production, and the downstream ribose branch remains the central weak point.
suggestions: Treat hydrothermal formaldehyde production as lower-confidence than formate generation
Strengthen transport rationale between vent and surface environments
Use sugar-fragment chemistry directly rather than requiring abundant free ribose

pathway_evaluations.feedback: A strong multi-environment route with a plausible surface adenine source and good environmental bridging from hydrothermal C1 feedstocks to surface assembly. Like other free-ribose-dependent pathways, it is weakened by the formose/ribose selectivity problem and by generic orthophosphate phosphorylation.
suggestions: Prefer DAP over generic orthophosphate for the adenosine to AMP step
Mark hydrothermal formaldehyde synthesis as tentative
Explicitly include borate mineral stabilization in the sugar branch

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This direct cTMP route to ATP is the weakest family in the network. Literature supports adenosine phosphorylation by trimetaphosphate under wet-dry cycles with some ATP formation, but making ATP directly from adenosine in one surface step is much less robust and bypasses the better-supported ADP intermediate required for the strongest ATP synthesis evidence. It is better treated as a low-confidence side route.
suggestions: Downgrade the direct adenosine-to-ATP route to speculative status
Route through AMP and ADP before ATP
Add Ni(II), wet-dry, and product-distribution caveats if retaining this step

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Compact but too compressed. The adenine and adenosine steps are plausible, but the direct conversion of adenosine to ATP via cTMP is not nearly as well supported as the ADP plus acetyl phosphate plus Fe3+ route and likely overstates selectivity and yield. This pathway reaches the target but with insufficient confidence.
suggestions: Expand through AMP and ADP intermediates
Use the acetyl phosphate/Fe3+ terminal step instead of direct cTMP ATP formation
Treat direct ATP formation from adenosine as minor-product chemistry only

evaluation.network_feedback: This is a strong, well-connected synthesis network that captures the current literature consensus that ATP prebiotic synthesis is best understood as a multi-environment assembly rather than a single continuous one-pot process. Topologically, the network is dense and connected, with appropriate hubs at adenine, ribose, adenosine, AMP, and ADP, and clear convergence from both HCN and formamide adenine routes as well as multiple nucleotide-assembly options. Environmental interconnection is a major strength: hydrothermal chemistry provides reduced carbon and acetyl phosphate-like energetic intermediates, surface chemistry builds nucleobases and sugar/nucleotide precursors, and biochemical completion uses the highly supported Fe3+/acetyl phosphate ATP-forming step. Literature alignment is generally good to very good, especially for DAMN/AICN adenine chemistry, formamide nucleobase synthesis, borate stabilization concepts, Ti-mineral-mediated adenosine formation, DAP/cTMP phosphorylation chemistry, and the Pinna et al. ATP terminal step. The main weaknesses are chemical rather than topological: the free-ribose/formose branch is overstated relative to current literature, ribose-1,2-cyclic phosphate formation is under-specified, adenosine-2'-phosphate rearrangement to AMP is simplified, hydrothermal acetyl phosphate generation from formate plus phosphate is not a strongly established direct reaction, and the direct cTMP route from adenosine to ATP is much weaker than the ADP-to-ATP route. Also, because the submitted summary omits detailed reaction metadata, it is impossible to fully verify that every hydrothermal and surface reaction actually specifies required mineral agents and literature citations; if absent in the full network JSON, that would be a substantial compliance issue. Overall, the network is scientifically thoughtful, mostly literature-aligned, and viable as an integrated hypothesis, but it falls short of approval at the >=0.9 threshold because several key bridging reactions remain speculative or oversimplified.
